factors.name: Economic Stress
factors.description: Measures the concentration of wealth, income disparities, and broad economic pressures on households. Elevated economic stress correlates with historical preconditions for mass unrest.

factors.name: Political Polarization
factors.description: Tracks the degree of ideological divergence between major political factions. High polarization reduces the possibility of negotiated resolution to grievances.

factors.name: Social Mobilization
factors.description: Tracks frequency, size, and geographic spread of organized public demonstrations and collective action. A leading indicator of mass mobilization capacity.

factors.name: Institutional Quality
factors.description: Measures public confidence in and functional capacity of government, judiciary, media, and law enforcement. Declining institutional quality removes the legitimating mechanisms that prevent political violence.

factors.name: Information & Media
factors.description: Tracks the health of the information ecosystem including media fragmentation, misinformation prevalence, and narrative control. A degraded information environment amplifies all other instability factors.
